description: Complete 48-week advanced 5-day Push/Pull/Legs/Upper/Lower hypertrophy program. 4 mesocycles of 2 blocks each, progressing through Hypertrophy Foundation (Meso 1, 8-12 reps), Strength-Hypertrophy (Meso 2, 6-10 reps), Metabolic Intensification (Meso 3, 10-15 reps), and Peak & Consolidation (Meso 4, 6-8 reps). Designed for advanced lifters training 5 days per week in a commercial gym.

exercises.notes: Set pulleys to chest height, slight forward lean at hips, maintain a fixed elbow angle throughout.
Brace the core, keep elbows pointing forward — don't let them flare wide.
Torso at ~45°, drive elbows back rather than pulling with the wrists.
Superset with Machine Lateral Raise. Set pulley at forehead height, external rotate at end of movement.
Superset with Cable Face Pull.
Upper arm stays fixed against the bench pad — don't let it swing forward at the top.
Start from knees, brace hard before rolling out, stop before hips drop.
Use a dip belt; go to full hang at the bottom for maximum lat stretch.
Anchor pulleys at hip height, drive up and across — emphasises upper chest and serratus.
Superset with Dumbbell Lateral Raise.
Superset with Cable Rear Delt Fly.
Rear foot elevated ~45cm. Upright torso = quad emphasis; forward lean = glute emphasis.
Use a padded barbell, drive through the heel, pause 1s at the top with full glute squeeze.
Superset with Cable Lateral Raise.
Superset with Rear Delt Machine.
If belt squat is unavailable, substitute Leg Press.
Use a box height that puts the working thigh parallel to the floor at the bottom. Drive through the heel of the elevated foot.
Kneel facing the stack, hold rope at forehead height, flex the spine downward — don't pull with arms.
Full hang at the bottom for maximum lat stretch. Add weight once you can exceed the top rep target across all sets.
If plate-loaded row is unavailable, substitute Chest-Supported Dumbbell Row.
Superset with Machine Lateral Raise. Brace against an incline bench to isolate the rear delts.
Superset with Dumbbell Rear Delt Fly.
Grip just inside shoulder width. Keep elbows tucked at ~45° to protect the wrists.
Use a dumbbell in the opposite hand to the working leg. Keep hips square throughout the movement.
